calendar.matters:
Petn. For Appt. Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-28-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa (Counsel for the Petitioner, Shirley Shaw).

The Court indicated that Mr. Wild (Counsel for the Trustee) submitted a settlement agreement with the status report. Mr. Siracusa stated for the record that his client has no objections to the settlement and withdrew the Petition for Order to Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; for Appointment of Successor Trustee; and for Order Setting Trustee's Bond. Consequently, the Court took the matter off calendar and signed Mr. Wild's Order Re: Petition to Bring Trust Under Court Supervision.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Maureen Howard CSR# 7156; Court Investigator: Mary Quinn; Bailiff: Douglas Handa; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.

Petn. For Appt. Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-29-2007 Continued To Jun-28-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lois Foster (Proposed Conservatee/Trustor); Trevor Roberts (Court-appointed Counsel for the Proposed Conservatee); Shirley Shaw (Petitioner); Douglas Penson (Counsel for Ms. Shaw); Maria Cazem (Objector/Trustee); Nelson Wild (Counsel for Ms. Cazem); Louis Johnson (Son/Interested Party); Mary Jane Gordon (Counsel for Mr. Johnson).

After noting that Mrs. Foster should not have been taken out of her home, Mr. Penson suggested the following: Continue the matter for further placement evaluation, refer the family to mediation for their communication issues, and bring the Trust under Court supervision in order to increase transparency and disclosure of estate information. Mr. Wild disagreed, noting that he opposed bringing the Trust under Court supervision, mediation is not necessary, Mrs. Foster's home is not safe in her condition, and that her current placement at Magnolia Gardens in Danville, CA was appropriate. Mr. Roberts reported that his client is content with her placement and wants to put an end to these proceedings. Mr. Roberts also noted that there is no evidence of misappropriation of funds, therefore, there is no need to bring the Trust under Court supervision. After extensive discussion regarding Mrs. Foster's property, Mr. Penson noted that the Interim Existing Order stated that her house could not be rented or occupied. The Court ordered that the Trustee (Maria Cazem) shall take the necessary steps to see that there is sufficient occupancy to protect the home without creating problems with tenancy. Mr. Wild is to prepare the Order for same. The Court also ordered that the family complete pro bono mediation (for Trust issues only) by June 22, 2007 (signing the Order for same in open court). The Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee was continued to June 28, 2007.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Lisa Schwam CSR#8994; Court Investigator: John Cushner; Bailiff: Henry; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.
